{\n139\t                    let running = running_flag.read().await;\n140\t                    if !*running {\n141\t                        break;\n142\t                    }\n143\t              warn!(\"Replica group {} not found in topology\", replica_group_id);\n205\t                return Ok(0);\n206\t            }\n207\t        };\n208\t\n209\t        // Mode A: Each pod sweeps only its rendezvous-owned shards\n210\t        // Build shard → node mapping using the same rendezvous pattern as query routing\n211\t        use crate::router;\n212\t        let mut rf);\n215\t            if replicas.is_empty() {\n216\t                continue;\n217\t            }\n218\t            // Use query_seq=0 for deterministic selection\n219\t            let selected = replicas[0].clone();\n220\t            shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n221\t        }\n222\t\n223\t        let mut total_deleted = 0u64;\n224\t        let mut pending_indexes = debug!(\"Reached max_deletes_per_sweep limit ({})\", max_deletes);\n230\t                break;\n231\t            }\n232\t\n233\t            let node = match topo.node(&node_id) {\n234\t                Some(n) => n,\n235\t                None => continue,\n236\t            };\n237\t\n238\t            // Build filter: _miroir_shard = {s} AND _miroir_expires_at <= {now_ms}\n239\t            let st.pending_indexes = pending_indexes;\n275\t        }\n276\t\n277\t        Ok(total_deleted)\n278\t    }\n279\t\n280\t    /// Get the current sweeper state.\n281\t    pub async fn state(&self) ->    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n66\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n67\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n68\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n69\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n70\t\n71\t        for resp in responses {\n72\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n73\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n74\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n75\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n76\t            }\n77\t        }\n78\t\n79\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n80\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n81\t        } else {\n82\t            0.0\n83\t        };\n84\t\n85\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n86\t        let terms = term_df\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n89\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n90\t                    0.0\n91\t                } else {\n92\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n93\t                };\n94\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n95\t            })\n96\t            .collect();\n97\t\n98\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n99\t    }\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\t// std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n245\t        async move {\n246\t            Ok(WriteResponse {\n247\t                success: false,\n248\t                task_uid: None,\n249\t                message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n250\t                code: None,\n251\t                error_type: None,\n252\t            })\n253\t        }\n254\t    }\n255\t\n256\t    /// Get task status from a node.\n257\t    fn get_task_status(\n258\t        &self,\n259\t        _node: &NodeId,\n260\t        _address: &str,\n261\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n262\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n263\t        async move {\n264\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n265\t                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n266\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n267\t                error: None,\n268\t                error_type: None,\n269\t            })\n270\t        }\n271\t    }\n272\t\n273\t    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n274\t    fn delete_documents(\n275\t        &self,\n276\t        _node: &NodeId,\n277\t        _address: &str,\n278\t        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n279\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n280\t        async move {\n281\t            Ok(DeleteResponse {\n282\t                success: false,\n283\t                task_uid: None,\n284\t                message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n285\t                code: None,\n286\t                error_type: None,\n287\t            })\n288\t        }\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n292\t    fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n293\t        &self,\n294\t        _node: &NodeId,\n295\t        _address: &str,\n296\t        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n297\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n298\t        async move {\n299\t            Ok(DeleteResponse {\n300\t                success: false,\n301\t                task_uid: None,\n302\t                message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n303\t                code: None,\n304\t                error_type: None,\n305\t            })\n306\t        }\n307\t    }\n308\t\n309\t    /// Fetch documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n310\t    fn fetch_documents(\n311\t        &self,\n312\t        _node: &NodeId,\n313\t        _address: &str,\n314\t        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n315\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n316\t        async move {\n317\t            Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n318\t                results: Vec::new(),\n319\t                limit: _request.limit,\n320\t                offset: _request.offset,\n321\t                total: 0,\n322\t            })\n323\t        }\n324\t    }\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n328\tpub enum NodeError {\n329\t    Timeout,\n330\t    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n331\t    NetworkError(String),\n332\t}\n333\t\n334\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n335\tpub struct SearchRequest {\n336\t    pub index_uid: String,\n337\t    pub query: Option<String>,\n338\t    pub offset: usize,\n339\t    pub limit: usize,\n340\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n341\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n342\t    pub ranking_score: bool,\n343\t    pub body: Value,\n344\t    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n345\t    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\timpl SearchRequest {\n349\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n350\t    ///\n351\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n352\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n353\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n354\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n355\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n356\t\n357\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n358\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n359\t\n360\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n361\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n362\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n363\t\n364\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n365\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n366\t\n367\t        // Ensure query is set\n368\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n369\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n370\t        }\n371\t\n372\t        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n373\t        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n374\t            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n375\t        }\n376\t\n377\t        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n378\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n379\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n380\t        }\n381\t\n382\t        body\n383\t    }\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t#[derive(Debug)]\n387\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n388\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n389\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n390\t    pub partial: bool,\n391\t    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n392\t}\n393\t\n394\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n395\tpub fn plan_search_scatter(\n396\t    topology: &Topology,\n397\t    query_seq: u64,\n398\t    rf: usize,\n399\t    shard_count: u32,\n400\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n401\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n402\t\n403\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n404\t        Some(g) => g,\n405\t        None => {\n406\t            return ScatterPlan {\n407\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n408\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n409\t            };\n410\t        }\n411\t    };\n412\t\n413\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n414\t\n415\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n416\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n417\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n418\t        let selected = replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone();\n419\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n420\t    }\n421\t\n422\t    ScatterPlan {\n423\t        chosen_group,\n424\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n425\t        shard_to_node,\n426\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n427\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n428\t    }\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n432\t///\n433\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n434\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n435\tpub fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n436\t    topology: &Topology,\n437\t    query_seq: u64,\n438\t    rf: usize,\n439\t    shard_count: u32,\n440\t    index: &str,\n441\t    floor: u64,\n442\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n443\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n444\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n445\t\n446\t    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n447\t\n448\t    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n449\t        shard_count,\n450\t        group,\n451\t        rf,\n452\t        query_seq,\n453\t        index,\n454\t        floor,\n455\t        version_checker,\n456\t    )?;\n457\t\n458\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n459\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n460\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n461\t        // Filter by version floor, then rotate by query_seq\n462\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n463\t            .iter()\n464\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n465\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n466\t                version >= floor\n467\t            })\n468\t            .collect();\n469\t\n470\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n471\t            return None;\n472\t        }\n473\t\n474\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n475\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n476\t    }\n477\t\n478\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n479\t        chosen_group,\n480\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n481\t        shard_to_node,\n482\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n483\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n484\t    })\n485\t}\n486\t\n487\t/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n488\t///\n489\t/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n490\t/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\n491\tpub fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n492\t    topology: &Topology,\n493\t    query_seq: u64,\n494\t    rf: usize,\n495\t    shard_count: u32,\n496\t    pinned_group: u32,\n497\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n498\t    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n499\t\n500\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n501\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n502\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n503\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n504\t            continue;\n505\t        }\n506\t        let selected = replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone();\n507\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n508\t    }\n509\t\n510\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n511\t        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n512\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n513\t        shard_to_node,\n514\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n515\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n516\t    })\n517\t}\n518\t\n519\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n520\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n521\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n522\t    client: &C,\n523\t    req: SearchRequest,\n524\t    topology: &Topology,\n525\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n526\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n527\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n528\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n529\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n530\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n531\t        }\n532\t    }\n533\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n534\t\n535\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n536\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n537\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n538\t\n539\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n540\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n541\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n542\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n543\t            None => {\n544\t                for shard_id in shards {\n545\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n546\t                }\n547\t                continue;\n548\t            }\n549\t        };\n550\t        let client_ref = client;\n551\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n552\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n553\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n554\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n555\t        let span = info_span!(\n556\t            \"scatter_node\",\n557\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n558\t            address = %node.address,\n559\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n560\t        );\n561\t        tasks.push(async move {\n562\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n563\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n564\t        }.instrument(span));\n565\t    }\n566\t\n567\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n568\t\n569\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n570\t        match result {\n571\t            Ok(body) => {\n572\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n573\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n574\t                }\n575\t            }\n576\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n577\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n578\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n579\t            }\n580\t            Err(e) => {\n581\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n582\t            }\n583\t        }\n584\t    }\n585\t\n586\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n587\t\n588\t    match policy {\n589\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n590\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n591\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n592\t            }\n593\t        }\n594\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n595\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n596\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n597\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n598\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n599\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n600\t\n601\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n602\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n603\t\n604\t                    // Try each other replica group\n605\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n606\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n607\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n608\t                        }\n609\t\n610\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n611\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n612\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n613\t                                continue;\n614\t                            }\n615\t\n616\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n617\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n618\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n619\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n620\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n621\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n622\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n623\t                                            break;\n624\t                                        }\n625\t                                        Err(_) => {\n626\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n627\t                                        }\n628\t                                    }\n629\t                                }\n630\t                            }\n631\t\n632\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n633\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n634\t                            }\n635\t                        }\n636\t                    }\n637\t\n638\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n639\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n640\t                    }\n641\t                }\n642\t\n643\t                // Merge fallback results\n644\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n645\t\n646\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n647\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n648\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n649\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n650\t                        shard_pages,\n651\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n652\t                        partial: false,\n653\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n654\t                    });\n655\t                } else {\n656\t                    // Some shards still failed\n657\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n658\t                }\n659\t            }\n660\t        }\n661\t    }\n662\t\n663\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n664\t}\n665\t\n666\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n667\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n668\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n669\t    client: &C,\n670\t    req: SearchRequest,\n671\t    topology: &Topology,\n672\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n673\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n674\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n675\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n676\t\n677\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n678\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n679\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n680\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n681\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n682\t            });\n683\t        }\n684\t    }\n685\t\n686\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n687\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n688\t\n689\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n690\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n691\t        offset: req.offset,\n692\t        limit: req.limit,\n693\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n694\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n695\t        failed_shards,\n696\t    };\n697\t\n698\t    // Span for the merge operation\n699\t    let _span = info_span!(\n700\t        \"merge\",\n701\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n702\t        offset = req.offset,\n703\t        limit = req.limit,\n704\t    ).entered();\n705\t\n706\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n710\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n711\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n712\t\n713\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n714\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n715\t    match query {\n716\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n717\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n718\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n719\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n720\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n721\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n722\t            }\n723\t            terms\n724\t        }\n725\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n726\t    }\n727\t}\n728\t\n729\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n730\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n731\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n732\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n733\t    client: &C,\n734\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n735\t    topology: &Topology,\n736\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n737\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n738\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n739\t    }\n740\t\n741\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n742\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n743\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n744\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n745\t        }\n746\t    }\n747\t\n748\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n749\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n750\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n751\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n752\t            None => continue,\n753\t        };\n754\t        let client_ref = client;\n755\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n756\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n757\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n758\t    }\n759\t\n760\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n761\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n762\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n763\t}\n764\t\n765\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n766\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n767\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n768\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n769\t    client: &C,\n770\t    req: SearchRequest,\n771\t    topology: &Topology,\n772\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n773\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n774\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n775\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n776\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n777\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n778\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n779\t    };\n780\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n781\t    let mut search_req = req;\n782\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n783\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n784\t}\n785\t\n786\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n787\t// Mock client\n788\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n789\t\n790\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n791\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n792\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n793\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n794\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n795\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n796\t}\n797\t\n798\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n799\t    fn search_node(\n800\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n801\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>> + Send {\n802\t        let node = node.clone();\n803\t        let err = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n804\t        let response = self.responses.get(&node).cloned();\n805\t        async move {\n806\t            if let Some(e) = err {\n807\t                return Err(e);\n808\t            }\n809\t            Ok(response.unwrap_or_else(|| {\n810\t                serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n811\t            }))\n812\t        }\n813\t    }\n814\t\n815\t    fn preflight_node(\n816\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n817\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n818\t        let node = node.clone();\n819\t        let err = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n820\t        let response = self.preflight_responses.get(&node).cloned();\n821\t        async move {\n822\t            if let Some(e) = err {\n823\t                return Err(e);\n824\t            }\n825\t            Ok(response.unwrap_or_else(|| {\n826\t                PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n827\t            }))\n828\t        }\n829\t    }\n830\t\n831\t    fn write_documents(\n832\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n833\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n834\t        let node = node.clone();\n835\t        let err = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n836\t        async move {\n837\t            if let Some(e) = err {\n838\t                return Err(e);\n839\t            }\n840\t            Ok(WriteResponse {\n841\t                success: true,\n842\t                task_uid: Some(1),\n843\t                message: None,\n844\t                code: None,\n845\t                error_type: None,\n846\t            })\n847\t        }\n848\t    }\n849\t\n850\t    fn delete_documents(\n851\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address:      })\n927\t        }\n928\t    }\n929\t}\n930\t\n931\t#[cfg(test)]\n932\tmod tests {\n933\t    use super::*;\n934\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n935\t\n936\t    fn make_test_topology() plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n1082\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1083\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1084\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1085\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n1086\t        });\n1087\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1088\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1089\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n1090\t        });\n1091\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1092\t            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n1093\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n1094\t        });\n1095\t        let req = PreflightRequest { 100000);\n1098\t        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n1099\t    }\n1100\t\n1101\t    #[tokio::test]\n1102\t    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n1103\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1104\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n1105\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1106\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n1107\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1108\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1109\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n1110\t        });\n1111\t        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1112\t        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n1113\t        assert!(!r.degraded);\n1114\t        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n1115\t    }\n1116\t\n1117\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n1118\t    ///\n1119\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n1120\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n1121\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n1122\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n1123\t    ///\n1124\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n1125\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1126\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1127\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n1128\t    ///\n1129\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n1130\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n1131\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1132\t    ///\n1133\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n1134\t    #[tokio::test]\n1135\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1136\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1137\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1138\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1139\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1140\t\n1141\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n1142\t\n1143\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n1144\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1145\t\n1146\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n1147\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1148\t            total_docs: 1000,\n1149\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1150\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1151\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1152\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1153\t            ]),\n1154\t        });\n1155\t\n1156\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1157\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1158\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n1159\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1160\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1161\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n1162\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n1163\t            ]),\n1164\t        });\n1165\t\n1166\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n1167\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1168\t            total_docs: 10,\n1169\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n1170\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1171\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1172\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1173\t            ]),\n1174\t        });\n1175\t\n1176\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n1177\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1178\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1179\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n1180\t            filter: None,\n1181\t        };\n1182\t\n1183\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1184\t\n1185\t        // Verify global aggregation\n1186\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n1187\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n1188\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n1189\t\n1190\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n1191\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n1192\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1193\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1194\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n1195\t\n1196\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1197\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1198\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1199\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1200\t\n1201\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1202\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1203\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1204\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n1205\t        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1206\t        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1207\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n1208\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n1209\t    }\n1210\t\n1211\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n1212\t    #[tokio::test]\n1213\t    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n1214\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1215\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n1216\t        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1217\t\n1218\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1219\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1220\t            terms: vec![],\n1221\t            filter: None,\n1222\t        };\n1223\t\n1224\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1225\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n1226\t        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n1227\t    }\n1228\t\n1229\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n1230\t    #[tokio::test]\n1231\t    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n1232\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1233\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1234\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1235\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1236\t\n1237\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n1238\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1239\t\n1240\t        // Node 0 returns valid data\n1241\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1242\t            total_docs: 50000,\n1243\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1244\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n1245\t        });\n1246\t\n1247\t        // Node 1 returns valid data\n1248\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1249\t            total_docs: 30000,\n1250\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1251\t            term_stats: Try to acquire leader lease for each index (scope: rebalance:<index>)\n287\t    /// 2. If acquired, process events and run migrations\n288\t    /// 3. Renew lease periodically\n289\t    /// 4. If lease lost, go back to step 1\n290\t    pub async fn run(&self) {\n291\t        info!(\n292\t            pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n293\t            \"rebalancer worker starting\"\n294\t        );\n295\t\n296\t        loop {\n297\t            // Try to acquire leader lease for each index we're managing\n298\t            let mut leader_scopes = Vec::new();\n299\t\n300\t            // Get all active indexes from current jobs and use default scope\n301\t            let jobs = self.jobs.read().await;\n302\t            let mut index_uids: Vec<String> = jobs.values()\n303\t                .map(|j| j.index_uid.clone())\n304\t                .collect();\n305\t\n306\t            // Always include \"default\" scope for rebalancer operations\n307\t            index_uids.push(\"default\".to_string());\n308\t            drop(jobs);\n309\t\n310\t            // Build scopes for each index: rebalance:<index>\n311\t            let scopes: Vec<String> = index_uids\n312\t                .into_iter()\n313\t                .map(|uid| format!(\"rebalance:{}\", uid))\n314\t                .collect();\n315\t\n316\t            let mut acquired_any = false;\n317\t            for scope in &scopes {\n318\t                let now_ms = now_ms();\n319\t                let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n320\t\n321\t                match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n322\t                    let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n323\t                    let scope = scope.clone();\n324\t                    let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n325\t                    move || {\n326\t                        task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at, now_ms)\n327\t                    }\n328\t                })\n329\t                .await\n330\t                {\n331\t                    Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n332\t                        info!(scope = %scope, pod_id = %self.pod_id, \"acquired leader lease\");\n333\t                        leader_scopes.push(scope.clone());\n334\t                        acquired_any = true;\n335\t                    }\n336\t                    Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n337\t                        debug!(scope = %scope, \"leader lease already held\");\n338\t                    }\n339\t                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n340\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to acquire leader lease\");\n341\t                    }\n342\t                    Err(e) => {\n343\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n344\t                    }\n345\t                }\n346\t            }\n347\t\n348\t            if acquired_any {\n349\t                // We are the leader - update rebalancer metrics\n350\t                {\n351\t                    let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n352\t                    metrics.start_rebalance();\n353\t                }\n354\t\n355\t                // Call metrics callback for rebalance start\n356\t                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_callback {\n357\t                    callback(true, None, None);\n358\t                }\n359\t\n360\t                // We are the leader - run the main loop\n361\t                if let Err(e) = self.run_leader_loop(&leader_scopes).await {\n362\t                    error!(error = %e, \"leader loop failed\");\n363\t                }\n364\t\n365\t                // Clear rebalancer in-progress status on exit\n366\t                {\n367\t                    let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n368\t                    metrics.end_rebalance();\n369\t                }\n370\t\n371\t                // Call metrics callback for rebalance end\n372\t                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_callback {\n373\t                    callback(false, None, None);\n374\t                }\n375\t            } else {\n376\t                // Not the leader - wait before retrying\n377\t                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n378\t                    self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n379\t                ))\n380\t                .await;\n381\t            }\n382\t        }\n383\t    }\n384\t\n385\t    /// Run the leader loop: process events, renew lease, drive migrations.\n386\t    async fn run_leader_loop(&self, scopes: &[String]) -> Result<(), String> {\n387\t        let mut lease_renewal = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n388\t            self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n389\t        ));\n390\t\n391\t        // Take the receiver out of the Option\n392\t        let mut event_rx = {\n393\t            let mut rx_guard = self.event_rx.write().await;\n394\t            rx_guard.take().ok_or_else(|| \"event receiver already taken\".to_string())?\n395\t        };\n396\t\n397\t        let result = async {\n398\t            loop {\n399\t                tokio::select! {\n400\t                    // Renew lease periodically\n401\t                    _ = lease_renewal.tick() => {\n402\t                        for scope in scopes {\n403\t                            let now_ms = now_ms();\n404\t                            let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n405\t\n406\t                            match tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n407\t                                let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n408\t                                let scope = scope.clone();\n409\t                                let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n410\t                                move || {\n411\t                                    task_store.renew_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at)\n412\t                                }\n413\t                            })\n414\t                            .await\n415\t                            {\n416\t                                Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n417\t                                    debug!(scope = %scope, \"renewed leader lease\");\n418\t                                }\n419\t                                Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n420\t                                    info!(scope = %scope, \"lost leader lease\");\n421\t                                    return Ok::<(), String>(()); // Exit loop, will retry acquisition\n422\t                                }\n423\t                                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n424\t                                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to renew lease\");\n425\t                                    return Err(format!(\"lease renewal failed: {}\", e));\n426\t                                }\n427\t                                Err(e) => {\n428\t                                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n429\t                                    return Err(format!(\"lease renewal task failed: {}\", e));\n430\t                                }\n431\t                            }\n432\t                        }\n433\t                    }\n434\t\n435\t                    // Process topology change events\n436\t                    Some(event) = event_rx.recv() => {\n437\t                        if let Err(e) = self.handle_topology_event(event).await {\n438\t                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to handle topology event\");\n439\t                        }\n440\t                    }\n441\t\n442\t                    // Drive active migrations\n443\t                    _ = tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)) => {\n444\t                        if let Err(e) = self.drive_migrations().await {\n445\t                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to drive migrations\");\n446\t                        }\n447\t                    }\n448\t                }\n449\t            }\n450\t        }.await;\n451\t\n452\t        // Put the receiver back for retry logic\n453\t        {\n454\t            let mut rx_guard = self.event_rx.write().await;\n455\t            if rx_guard.is_none() {\n456\t                *rx_guard = Some(event_rx);\n457\t            }\n458\t        }\n459\t\n460\t        result\n461\t    }\n462\t\n463\t    /// Handle a topology change event.\n464\t    ///\n465\t    /// This method verifies that this pod is the leader before processing\n466\t    /// the event. If not the leader, it returns an error without creating\n467\t    /// any migrations.\n468\t    pub async fn handle_topology_event(&self, event: TopologyChangeEvent) -> Result<(), String> {\n469\t        info!(event = ?event, \"handling topology change event\");\n470\t\n471\t        // Derive the scope from the event to check leadership\n472\t        let scope = match &event {\n473\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeAdded { index_uid, .. } => format!(\"rebalance:{}\", index_uid),\n474\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeDraining { index_uid, .. } => format!(\"rebalance:{}\", index_uid),\n475\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeFailed { index_uid, .. } => format!(\"rebalance:{}\", index_uid),\n476\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeRecovered { index_uid, .. } => format!(\"rebalance:{}\", index_uid),\n477\t        };\n478\t\n479\t        // Compute lease expiration before spawning\n480\t        let now_ms = now_ms();\n481\t        let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n482\t\n483\t        // Check if we are the leader for this scope\n484\t        let is_leader = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n485\t            let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n486\t            let scope = scope.clone();\n487\t            let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n488\t            move || {\n489\t                // Try to acquire - if we already hold it, this succeeds\n490\t                // If we don't hold it, this fails\n491\t                task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at, now_ms)\n492\t            }\n493\t        })\n494\t        .await\n495\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to check leader lease: {}\", e))?\n496\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to check leader lease: {}\", e))?;\n497\t\n498\t        if !is_leader {\n499\t            debug!(\n500\t                scope = %scope,\n501\t                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n502\t                \"not the leader, skipping topology event (another pod will handle it)\"\n503\t            );\n504\t            // Return Ok - not being leader is not an error, just means another pod handles it\n505\t            return Ok(());\n506\t        }\n507\t\n508\t        // Now process the event (we own it now after deriving scope)\n509\t        match event {\n510\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeAdded {\n511\t                node_id,\n512\t                replica_group,\n513\t                index_uid,\n514\t            } => {\n515\t                self.on_node_added(&node_id, replica_group, &index_uid)\n516\t                    .await?\n517\t            }\n518\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeDraining {\n519\t                node_id,\n520\t                replica_group,\n521\t                index_uid,\n522\t            } => {\n523\t                self.on_node_draining(&node_id, replica_group, &index_uid)\n524\t                    .await?\n525\t            }\n526\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeFailed {\n527\t                node_id,\n528\t                replica_group,\n529\t                index_uid,\n530\t            } => {\n531\t                self.on_node_failed(&node_id, replica_group, &index_uid)\n532\t                    .await?\n533\t            }\n534\t            TopologyChangeEvent::NodeRecovered {\n535\t                node_id,\n536\t                replica_group,\n537\t                index_uid,\n538\t            } => {\n539\t                self.on_node_recovered(&node_id, replica_group, &index_uid)\n540\t                    .await?\n541\t            }\n542\t        }\n543\t\n544\t        Ok(())\n545\t    }\n546\t\n547\t    /// Handle node addition: compute affected shards and create job to track migration.\n548\t    async fn on_node_added(\n549\t        &self,\n550\t        node_id: &str,\n551\t        replica_group: u32,\n552\t        index_uid: &str,\n553\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n554\t        let job_id = RebalanceJobId::new(index_uid);\n555\t\n556\t        // Check if we already have a job for this index in memory\n557\t        {\n558\t            let jobs = self.jobs.read().await;\n559\t            if jobs.contains_key(&job_id) {\n560\t                debug!(index_uid = %index_uid, \"rebalance job already exists\");\n561\t                return Ok(());\n562\t            }\n563\t        }\n564\t\n565\t        // Also check the task store for existing jobs (from other workers)\n566\t        let existing_jobs = tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n567\t            let task_store = self.task_store.clone();\n568\t            move || {\n569\t                task_store.list_jobs_by_state(\"running\")\n570\t            }\n571\t        })\n572\t        .await\n573\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to list jobs: {}\", e))?\n574\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to list jobs: {}\", e))?;\n575\t\n576\t        for existing_job in existing_jobs {\n577\t            if existing_job.id == job_id.0 {\n578\t                debug!(\n579\t                    index_uid = %index_uid,\n580\t                    \"rebalance job already exists in task store\"\n581\t                );\n582\t                return Ok(());\n583\t            }\n584\t        }\n585\t\n586\t        // Compute affected shards using the Phase 1 router\n587\t        let affected_shards = self.compute_affected_shards_for_add(node_id, replica_group).await?;\n588\t\n589\t        if affected_shards.is_empty() {\n590\t            info!(\n591\t                node_id = %node_id,\n592\t                replica_group = replica_group,\n593\t                \"no shards need migration for node addition\"\n594\t            );\n595\t            return Ok(());\n596\t        }\n597\t\n598\t        info!(\n599\t            node_id = %node_id,\n600\t            replica_group = replica_group,\n601\t            shard_count = affected_shards.len(),\n602\t            \"computed affected shards for node addition\"\n603\t        );\n604\t\n605\t        // Build migration state: shard -> old owner mapping\n606\t        let mut old_owners = HashMap::new();\n607\t        let mut shard_states = HashMap::new();\n608\t        for (shard_id, source_node) in &affected_shards {\n609\t            old_owners.insert(ShardId(*shard_id), topo_to_migration_node_id(source_node));\n610\t            shard_states.insert(\n611\t                *shard_id,\n612\t                ShardState {\n613\t                    phase: ShardMigrationPhase::Idle,\n614\t                    docs_migrated: 0,\n615\t                    last_offset: 0,\n616\t                    source_node: Some(source_node.to_string()),\n617\t                    target_node: node_id.to_string(),\n618\t                    started_at: Instant::now(),\n619\t                },\n620\t            );\n621\t        }\n622\t\n623\t        // Create migration in coordinator for state tracking and dual-write\n624\t        let migration_id = {\n625\t            let mut coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.write().await;\n626\t            let new_node = topo_to_migration_node_id(&TopologyNodeId::new(node_id.to_string()));\n627\t            coordinator.begin_migration(new_node, replica_group, old_owners)\n628\t                .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to create migration: {}\", e))?\n629\t        };\n630\t\n631\t        // Start dual-write immediately so the router starts writing to both nodes\n632\t        {\n633\t            let mut coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.write().await;\n634\t            coordinator.begin_dual_write(migration_id)\n635\t                .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to start dual-write: {}\", e))?;\n636\t        }\n637\t\n638\t        let job = RebalanceJob {\n639\t            id: job_id.clone(),\n640\t            index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n641\t            replica_group,\n642\t            shards: shard_states,\n643\t            started_at: Instant::now(),\n644\t            completed_at: None,\n645\t            total_docs_migrated: 0,\n646\t            paused: false,\n647\t        };\n648\t\n649\t        // Persist job to task store\n650\t        self.persist_job(&job).await?;\n651\t\n652\t        // Store in memory\n653\t        let mut jobs = self.jobs.write().await;\n654\t        jobs.insert(job_id.clone(), job);\n655\t\n656\t        info!(\n657\t            migration_id = %migration_id,\n658\t            shard_count = affected_shards.len(),\n659\t            \"created migration for node addition\"\n660\t        );\n661\t\n662\t        Ok(())\n663\t    }\n664\t\n665\t    /// Handle node draining: compute destination shards and create job to track migration.\n666\t    async fn on_node_draining(\n667\t        &self,\n668\t        node_id: &str,\n669\t        replica_group: u32,\n670\t        index_uid: &str,\n671\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n672\t        let job_id = RebalanceJobId::new(index_uid);\n673\t\n674\t        // Compute shard destinations\n675\t        let shard_destinations = self\n676\t            .compute_shard_destinations_for_drain(node_id, replica_group)\n677\t            .await?;\n678\t\n679\t        if shard_destinations.is_empty() {\n680\t            info!(\n681\t                node_id = %node_id,\n682\t                replica_group = replica_group,\n683\t                \"no shards need migration for node drain\"\n684\t            );\n685\t            return Ok(());\n686\t        }\n687\t\n688\t        info!(\n689\t            node_id = %node_id,\n690\t            replica_group = replica_group,\n691\t            shard_count = shard_destinations.len(),\n692\t            \"computed shard destinations for node drain\"\n693\t        );\n694\t\n695\t        // Build migration state: shard -> old owner (draining node) mapping\n696\t        let mut old_owners = HashMap::new();\n697\t        let mut shard_states = HashMap::new();\n698\t        for (shard_id, dest_node) in &shard_destinations {\n699\t            old_owners.insert(ShardId(*shard_id), topo_to_migration_node_id(&TopologyNodeId::new(node_id.to_string())));\n700\t            shard_states.insert(\n701\t                *shard_id,\n702\t                ShardState {\n703\t                    phase: ShardMigrationPhase::Idle,\n704\t                    docs_migrated: 0,\n705\t                    last_offset: 0,\n706\t                    source_node: Some(node_id.to_string()),\n707\t                    target_node: dest_node.to_string(),\n708\t                    started_at: Instant::now(),\n709\t                },\n710\t            );\n711\t        }\n712\t\n713\t        // Create migration in coordinator for state tracking and dual-write\n714\t        let migration_id = {\n715\t            let mut coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.write().await;\n716\t            // For drain, the destination node becomes the \"new\" node in the migration\n717\t            if let Some((_, first_dest)) = shard_destinations.first() {\n718\t                let new_node = topo_to_migration_node_id(first_dest);\n719\t                coordinator.begin_migration(new_node, replica_group, old_owners)\n720\t                    .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to create migration: {}\", e))?\n721\t            } else {\n722\t                return Err(\"no shards to migrate\".to_string());\n723\t            }\n724\t        };\n725\t\n726\t        // Start dual-write immediately\n727\t        {\n728\t            let mut coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.write().await;\n729\t            coordinator.begin_dual_write(migration_id)\n730\t                .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to start dual-write: {}\", e))?;\n731\t        }\n732\t\n733\t        let job = RebalanceJob {\n734\t            id: job_id.clone(),\n735\t            index_uid: index_uid.to_string(),\n736\t            replica_group,\n737\t            shards: shard_states,\n738\t            started_at: Instant::now(),\n739\t            completed_at: None,\n740\t            total_docs_migrated: 0,\n741\t            paused: false,\n742\t        };\n743\t\n744\t        // Persist job to task store\n745\t        self.persist_job(&job).await?;\n746\t\n747\t        // Store in memory\n748\t        let mut jobs = self.jobs.write().await;\n749\t        jobs.insert(job_id.clone(), job);\n750\t\n751\t        info!(\n752\t            migration_id = %migration_id,\n753\t            shard_count = shard_destinations.len(),\n754\t            \"created migration for node drain\"\n755\t        );\n756\t\n757\t        Ok(())\n758\t    }\n759\t\n760\t    /// Handle node failure.\n761\t    async fn on_node_failed(\n762\t        &self,\n763\t        node_id: &str,\n764\t        replica_group: u32,\n765\t        index_uid: &str,\n766\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n767\t        info!(\n768\t            node_id = %node_id,\n769\t            replica_group = replica_group,\n770\t            index_uid = %index_uid,\n771\t            \"handling node failure\"\n772\t        );\n773\t\n774\t        // Mark node as failed in topology\n775\t        let node_id_obj = TopologyNodeId::new(node_id.to_string());\n776\t        {\n777\t            let mut topo = self.topology.write().await;\n778\t            if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(&node_id_obj) {\n779\t                node.status = crate::topology::NodeStatus::Failed;\n780\t            }\n781\t        }\n782\t\n783\t        // TODO: Schedule replication to restore RF if needed\n784\t        // For now, just log the failure\n785\t        Ok(())\n786\t    }\n787\t\n788\t    /// Handle node recovery.\n789\t    async fn on_node_recovered(\n790\t        &self,\n791\t        node_id: &str,\n792\t        replica_group: u32,\n793\t        index_uid: &str,\n794\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n795\t        info!(\n796\t            node_id = %node_id,\n797\t            replica_group = replica_group,\n798\t            index_uid = %index_uid,\n799\t            \"handling node recovery\"\n800\t        );\n801\t\n802\t        // Mark node as active in topology\n803\t        let node_id_obj = TopologyNodeId::new(node_id.to_string());\n804\t        {\n805\t            let mut topo = self.topology.write().await;\n806\t            if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(&node_id_obj) {\n807\t                node.status = crate::topology::NodeStatus::Active;\n808\t            }\n809\t        }\n810\t\n811\t        // TODO: If auto_rebalance_on_recovery is enabled, trigger rebalancing\n812\t\n813\t        Ok(())\n814\t    }\n815\t\n816\t    /// Compute which shards are affected by adding a new node.\n817\t    /// Returns shard -> source_node mapping for shards that will move.\n818\t    async fn compute_affected_shards_for_add(\n819\t        &self,\n820\t        new_node_id: &str,\n821\t        replica_group: u32,\n822\t    ) -> Result<Vec<(u32, TopologyNodeId)>, String> {\n823\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n824\t        let new_node_id = TopologyNodeId::new(new_node_id.to_string());\n825\t        let rf = topo.rf();\n826\t\n827\t        // Find the target group\n828\t        let group = topo\n829\t            .groups()\n830\t            .find(|g| g.id == replica_group)\n831\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"replica group {} not found\", replica_group))?;\n832\t\n833\t        let existing_nodes: Vec<_> = group.nodes().iter().cloned().collect();\n834\t        let mut affected_shards = Vec::new();\n835\t\n836\t        // For each shard, check if adding the new node would change the assignment\n837\t        for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n838\t            let old_assignment: Vec<_> =\n839\t                assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &existing_nodes, rf);\n840\t\n841\t            // New assignment with the new node included\n842\t            let all_nodes: Vec<_> = existing_nodes\n843\t                .iter()\n844\t                .cloned()\n845\t                .chain(std::iter::once(new_node_id.clone()))\n846\t                .collect();\n847\t            let new_assignment: Vec<_> = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &all_nodes, rf);\n848\t\n849\t            // Check if the new node is in the new assignment\n850\t            if new_assignment.contains(&new_node_id) {\n851\t                // This shard moves to the new node\n852\t                if let Some(old_owner) = old_assignment.first() {\n853\t                    affected_shards.push((shard_id, old_owner.clone()));\n854\t                }\n855\t            }\n856\t        }\n857\t\n858\t        Ok(affected_shards)\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    /// Compute where each shard should go when draining a node.\n862\t    /// Returns shard -> destination_node mapping.\n863\t    async fn compute_shard_destinations_for_drain(\n864\t        &self,\n865\t        drain_node_id: &str,\n866\t        replica_group: u32,\n867\t    ) -> Result<Vec<(u32, TopologyNodeId)>, String> {\n868\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n869\t        let drain_node_id = TopologyNodeId::new(drain_node_id.to_string());\n870\t        let rf = topo.rf();\n871\t\n872\t        // Find the target group\n873\t        let group = topo\n874\t            .groups()\n875\t            .find(|g| g.id == replica_group)\n876\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"replica group {} not found\", replica_group))?;\n877\t\n878\t        let other_nodes: Vec<_> = group\n879\t            .nodes()\n880\t            .iter()\n881\t            .filter(|n| **n != drain_node_id)\n882\t            .cloned()\n883\t            .collect();\n884\t\n885\t        if other_nodes.is_empty() {\n886\t            return Err(\"cannot remove last node in group\".to_string());\n887\t        }\n888\t\n889\t        let mut destinations = Vec::new();\n890\t\n891\t        // For each shard, find a new owner among the remaining nodes\n892\t        for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n893\t            let assignment: Vec<_> = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n894\t\n895\t            if assignment.contains(&drain_node_id) {\n896\t                // This shard needs a new home\n897\t                let mut best_node = None;\n898\t                let mut best_score = 0u64;\n899\t\n900\t                for node in &other_nodes {\n901\t                    let s = crate::router::score(shard_id, node.as_str());\n902\t                    if s > best_score {\n903\t                        best_score = s;\n904\t                        best_node = Some(node.clone());\n905\t                    }\n906\t                }\n907\t\n908\t                if let Some(dest) = best_node {\n909\t                    destinations.push((shard_id, dest));\n910\t                }\n911\t            }\n912\t        }\n913\t\n914\t        Ok(destinations)\n915\t    }\n916\t\n917\t    /// Drive active migrations forward.\n918\t    async fn drive_migrations(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n919\t        let jobs = self.jobs.read().await;\n920\t        let mut active_jobs = Vec::new();\n921\t\n922\t        for (job_id, job) in jobs.iter() {\n923\t            if job.paused || job.completed_at.is_some() {\n924\t                continue;\n925\t            }\n926\t\n927\t            // Count how many shards are actively migrating\n928\t            let migrating_count = job\n929\t                .shards\n930\t                .values()\n931\t                .filter(|s| {\n932\t                    matches!(\n933\t                        s.phase,\n934\t                        ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationInProgress\n935\t                            | ShardMigrationPhase::DualWriteStarted\n936\t                    )\n937\t                })\n938\t                .count();\n939\t\n940\t            if migrating_count < self.config.max_concurrent_migrations as usize {\n941\t                active_jobs.push((job_id.clone(), job.clone()));\n942\t            }\n943\t        }\n944\t\n945\t        // Drop read lock before processing\n946\t        drop(jobs);\n947\t\n948\t        // Process up to max_concurrent_migrations jobs\n949\t        for (job_id, job) in active_jobs\n950\t            .into_iter()\n951\t            .take(self.config.max_concurrent_migrations as usize)\n952\t        {\n953\t            if let Err(e) = self.process_job(&job_id).await {\n954\t                error!(job_id = %job_id.0, error = %e, \"failed to process job\");\n955\t            }\n956\t        }\n957\t\n958\t        Ok(())\n959\t    }\n960\t\n961\t    /// Emit Prometheus metrics for the current rebalancer state.\n962\t    pub async fn emit_metrics(&self) {\n963\t        let jobs = self.jobs.read().await;\n964\t\n965\t        // Calculate total documents migrated across all jobs\n966\t        let total_docs: u64 = jobs.values()\n967\t            .map(|j| j.total_docs_migrated)\n968\t            .sum();\n969\t\n970\t        // Check if any rebalance is in progress\n971\t        let in_progress = jobs.values().any(|j| j.completed_at.is_none() && !j.paused);\n972\t\n973\t        drop(jobs);\n974\t\n975\t        // Update internal metrics\n976\t        {\n977\t            let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n978\t            if in_progress {\n979\t                metrics.start_rebalance();\n980\t            } else {\n981\t                metrics.end_rebalance();\n982\t            }\n983\t            // Note: documents_migrated_total is already tracked in RebalancerMetrics\n984\t            // and synced to Prometheus via the health checker\n985\t            let _ = total_docs;\n986\t        }\n987\t\n988\t        // Call metrics callback for rebalance status\n989\t        if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_callback {\n990\t            callback(in_progress, None, None);\n991\t        }\n992\t    }\n993\t\n994\t    /// Get the current rebalancer status for monitoring.\n995\t    pub async fn get_status(&self) -> RebalancerWorkerStatus {\n996\t        let jobs = self.jobs.read().await;\n997\t\n998\t        let active_jobs = jobs.values()\n999\t            .filter(|j| j.completed_at.is_none() && !j.paused)\n1000\t            .count();\n1001\t\n1002\t .filter(|j| j.paused)\n1008\t            .count();\n1009\t\n1010\t        let total_shards: usize = jobs.values()\n1011\t            .map(|j| j.shards.len())\n1012\t            .sum();\n1013\t\n1014\t .sum();\n1017\t\n1018\t        RebalancerWorkerStatus {\n1019\t            active_jobs,\n1020\t            completed_jobs,\n1021\t            paused_jobs,\n1022\t            total_shards,\n1023\t            completed_shards,\n1024\t        }\n1025\t    }\n1026\t\n1027\t    /// Process a single rebalance job.\n1028\t    ///\n1029\t    /// Drives the migration state machine forward for each shard in the job.\n1030\t    /// This is the core method that advances migrations through their phases.\n1031\t    async fn process_job(&self, job_id: &RebalanceJobId) -> Result<(), String> {\n1032\t        // Get job (cloned to avoid holding lock)\n1033\t        let job = {\n1034\t            let jobs = self.jobs.read().await;\n1035\t            jobs.get(job_id).cloned()\n1036\t        };\n1037\t\n1038\t        let mut job = match job {\n1039\t            Some(j) => j,\n1040\t            None => return Ok(()), // Job may have been removed\n1041\t        };\n1042\t\n1043\t        // Skip paused or completed jobs\n1044\t        if job.paused || job.completed_at.is_some() {\n1045\t            return Ok(());\n1046\t        }\n1047\t\n1048\t        // Sync worker job state with MigrationCoordinator state\n1049\t        // This ensures we resume from the correct phase after a pod restart\n1050\t        self.sync_job_with_coordinator(&mut job).await?;\n1051\t\n1052\t        // Get the migration from the coordinator for this job\n1053\t        let migration_id = {\n1054\t            let coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.read().await;\n1055\t            let mut found_id = None;\n1056\t            for (mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n1057\t                // Match by index_uid and replica_group\n1058\t                if state.replica_group == job.replica_group {\n1059\t                    found_id = Some(*mid);\n1060\t                    break;\n1061\t                }\n1062\t            }\n1063\t            found_id.ok_or_else(|| \"no migration found for this job\".to_string())?\n1064\t        };\n1065\t\n1066\t        // Get migration state to access node addresses\n1067\t        let (new_node, old_owners) = {\n1068\t            let coordinator =          (state.new_node.clone(), state.old_owners.clone())\n1072\t        };\n1073\t\n1074\t        // Get node addresses from topology\n1075\t        let (new_node_address, old_owner_addresses) = {\n1076\t            let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n1077\t            let new_addr = topo.node(&migration_to_topo_node_id(&new_node))\n1078\t                .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"new node    }\n1087\t\n1088\t            (new_addr, old_addrs)\n1089\t        };\n1090\t\n1091\t        // Use a default index for now - in production, this would come from config\n1092\t        let index_uid = \"default\".to_string();\n1093\t\n1094\t        // Drive migrations forward for each shard\n1095\t        let mut updated = false;\n1096\t        let mut total_docs_migrated = 0u64;\n1097\t\n1098\t        // Limit concurrent migrations to stay within memory budget\n1099\t        let mut active_count = 0;\n1100\t\n1101\t        for (&shard_id, shard_state) in job.shards.iter_mut() {\n1102\t            // Check concurrent migration limit\n1103\t            if active_count >= self.config.max_concurrent_migrations as usize {\n1104\t                break;\n1105\t            }\n1106\t\n1107\t            match shard_state.phase {\n1108\t                ShardMigrationPhase::Idle => {\n1109\t                    // Already started dual-write in on_node_added/on_node_draining\n1110\t                    shard_state.phase = ShardMigrationPhase::DualWriteStarted;\n1111\t                    updated = true;\n1112\t                }\n1113\t                ShardMigrationPhase::DualWriteStarted => {\n1114\t                    // Start background migration\n1115\t                    if let Some(ref executor) = self.migration_executor {\n1116\t                        if let Some(old_address) =                   .unwrap_or_else(|| crate::migration::NodeId(\"unknown\".to_string()));\n1120\t                            if let Err(e) = self.execute_background_migration(\n1121\t               active_count += 1;\n1135\t                                updated = true;\n1136\t                            }\n1137\t                        }\n1138\t                    } else {\n1139\t                        // No executor - skip directly to complete for testing\n1140\t                        shard_state.docs_migrated = 1000; // Simulated\n1141\t                        shard_state.phase = ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationComplete;\n1142\t                        updated = true;\n1143\t                    }\n1144\t                }\n1145\t                ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationInProgress => {\n1146\t                    // Check if migration is complete by querying the coordinator\n1147\t                    let complete = self.check_migration_complete_for_shard(shard_id).await?;\n1148\t                    if complete {\n1149\t                        shard_state.phase = ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationComplete;\n1150\t                        active_count -= 1; // One less active migration\n1151\t                        updated = true;\n1152\t                    }\n1153\t                }\n1154\t                ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationComplete => {\n1155\t                    // Begin cutover sequence\n1156\t                    if let Err(e) = self.begin_cutover_for_shard(shard_id).await {\n1157\t                        error!(shard_id, error = %e, \"failed to begin cutover\");\n1158\t                    } else {\n1159\t                        shard_state.phase = error!(shard_id, error = %e, \"failed to complete cutover\");\n1167\t                    } else {\n1168\t                        shard_state.phase = ShardMigrationPhase::OldReplicaDeleted;\n1169\t                        updated = true;\n1170\t                    }\n1171\t                }\n1172\t                ShardMigrationPhase::OldReplicaDeleted => {\n1173\t                    // Migration }\n1178\t            }\n1179\t\n1180\t            total_docs_migrated += shard_state.docs_migrated;\n1181\t        }\n1182\t\n1183\t        // Update total docs migrated for the job\n1184\t info!(\n1211\t                    job_id = %job_id.0,\n1212\t                    duration_secs = duration,\n1213\t                    \"rebalance job completed\"\n1214\t                );\n1215\t            }\n1216\t\n1217\t            // Call metrics callback for rebalance completion with duration\n1218\t            if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_callback {\n1219\t Ok(())\n1236\t    }\n1237\t\n1238\t    /// Persist a job to the task store.\n1239\t    async fn persist_job(&self, job: &RebalanceJob) -> Result<(), String> {\n1240\t        let progress =            type_: \"rebalance\".to_string(),\n1246\t            params: progress,\n1247\t            state: if job.completed_at.is_some() {\n1248\t                \"completed\".to_string()\n1249\t async fn persist_job_progress(\n1285\t        &self,\n1286\t        job: &RebalanceJob,\n1287\t        shard_id: u32,\n1288\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n1289\t        if let Some(shard_state) =  .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e))?;\n1302\t\n1303\t            // Update job progress in task store\n1304\t            tokio::task::spawn_blocking({\n1305\t     }\n1312\t            })\n1313\t            .await\n1314\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to update job progress: {}\", e))?\n1315\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"failed to update job progress: {}\", e))?;\n1316\t        }\n1317\t\n1318\t        Ok(())\n1319\t    }\n1320\t\n1321\t    /// Sync worker job state with MigrationCoordinator state.\n1322\t    ///\n1323\t    /// This ensures that after a pod restart, the worker's job state reflects\n1324\t    /// the actual migration state tracked by the coordinator.\n1325\t    async fn sync_job_with_coordinator(&self, job: &mut RebalanceJob) -> Result<(), String> {\n1326\t        let coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.read().await;\n1327\t\n1328\t        // For each shard in the job, check if there's a corresponding migration\n1329\t        // in the coordinator and sync the state\n1330\t        for (&shard_id, shard_state) in job.shards.iter_mut() {\n1331\t            let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n1332\t\n1333\t            // Look for a migration in the coordinator that affects this shard\n1334\t            for (_mid, migration_state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n1335\t                if let Some(migration_shard_state) = migration_state.affected_shards.get(&shard) {\n1336\t                    // Sync the phase based on the migration coordinator state\n1337\t                    use crate::migration::ShardMigrationState as CoordinatorState;\n1338\t                    shard_state.phase = match migration_shard_state {\n1339\t                        CoordinatorState::Pending => ShardMigrationPhase::Idle,\n1340\t                        CoordinatorState::Migrating { .. } => ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationInProgress,\n1341\t                        CoordinatorState::MigrationComplete { docs_copied } => {\n1342\t                            shard_state.docs_migrated = *docs_copied;\n1343\t                            ShardMigrationPhase::MigrationComplete\n1344\t                        }\n1345\t                        CoordinatorState::Draining { .. } => ShardMigrationPhase::DualWriteStopped,\n1346\t                        CoordinatorState::DeltaPass { docs_copied, delta_docs_copied } => {\n1347\t                            shard_state.docs_migrated = docs_copied + delta_docs_copied;\n1348\t                            ShardMigrationPhase::DualWriteStopped\n1349\t                        }\n1350\t                        CoordinatorState::Active => ShardMigrationPhase::OldReplicaDeleted,\n1351\t                        CoordinatorState::Failed { .. } => ShardMigrationPhase::Failed,\n1352\t                    };\n1353\t                }\n1354\t            }\n1355\t        }\n1356\t\n1357\t        Ok(())\n1358\t    }\n1359\t\n1360\t    /// Start dual-write phase for a shard.\n1361\t    async fn start_dual_write_for_shard(&self, _replica_group: u32, shard_id: u32) -> Result<(), String> {\n1362\t        let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n1363\t        let mut coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.write().await;\n1364\t\n1365\t        // Find or create the migration for this shard\n1366\t        // For now, we'll create a new migration if one doesn't exist\n1367\t        // In production, this would be created when the job is created\n1368\t\n1369\t        info!(\n1370\t            shard_id,\n1371\t            \"starting dual-write phase\"\n1372\t        );\n1373\t\n1374\t        // The dual-write is handled by the router checking is_dual_write_active\n1375\t        // We just need to ensure the migration coordinator knows about this shard\n1376\t        Ok(())\n1377\t    }\n1378\t\n1379\t    /// Begin cutover sequence for a shard.\n1380\t    async fn begin_cutover_for_shard(&self, shard_id: u32) -> Result<(), String> {\n1381\t        info!(\n1382\t            shard_id,\n1383\t            \"beginning cutover sequence\"\n1384\t        );\n1385\t\n1386\t        let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n1387\t        let mut coordinator = self.migration_coordinator.write().await;\n1388\t\n1389\t        // Collect the migrations that affect this shard first\n1390\t        let migrations_to_cutover: Vec<_> = coordinator.get_all_migrations()\n1391\t            .iter()\n1392\t            .filter(|(_, migration_state)| migration_state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard))\n1393\t            .map(|(mid, _)| *mid)\n1394\t            .collect();\n1395\t\n1396\t        // Now perform the cutover\n1397\t        for mid
